summary: wellnpm ships a Monero cryptominer (xmrig) hardcoded to the author's wallet
details: The package's `main` and `start` entrypoint is a 24MB ELF binary that is xmrig, the Monero cryptocurrency miner. `config.json` hardcodes the mining pool `pool.hashvault.pro:80` and a Monero wallet address belonging to the package author (`4AUTfJGTvT8A98PSE99EunfrnUbkWe9vU3FN3qsABQLP5aPKQuvWS5sCaD9WaXFQUqNtDLb9dcxiBgJiWCfjvDXp5ptWDYG`). Running `npm start` or executing `./launch` hijacks the installer's CPU/electricity to mine XMR to the author's wallet. The `package.json` `deploy` script additionally moves `libuv.so` into `/data/data/com.termux/files/usr/lib`, chmods `xmr`/`edit` executable, copies them to `/data/data/com.termux/files/usr/bin`, and stages them in `$HOME`, persistently wiring the miner into the user's Termux shell PATH so typing `xmr` re-launches mining via `node_modules/wellnpm`. Strings in `launch` (`https://xmrig.com/wizard`, `https://xmrig.com/benchmark/`, RandomX/cn algorithm names) confirm the binary identity. Package metadata is generic placeholder content (`author: "Your Name <wellenterprise81.gmail.com>"`, description `"A brief description of your package"`, README is a hello-world stub) acting as cover for the miner payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
